text: Queen’s University welcomes graduate students from over 70 countries, enriching the community with diverse cultures and experiences. While application requirements are largely similar to domestic students, international applicants have additional considerations. Prospective students should consult the How to Apply page and the Academic Calendar for important funding deadlines and key dates.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: New international graduate students are encouraged to participate in the International Student Summer Orientation offered through the Queen’s University International Centre (QUIC). Students may also consider the Graduate Transition and Academic Success (GTAS) program to support their academic integration.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s provides extensive resources tailored to international students. The International Student Guidebook covers immigration, housing, and health insurance. The Queen’s University International Centre (QUIC) offers culturally sensitive programming and support. Student Academic Success Services (SASS) provides language workshops, dissertation boot camps, and writing support. Career Services includes dedicated Career Educators and Coaches for International Students. The Ban Righ Centre fosters community and support for women-identified students. Graduate Inclusivity Fellows (GIFs) connect prospective and current students to discuss life as an international graduate student.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: International students are encouraged to explore Queen’s funding packages, entrance scholarships, fellowships, and bursaries through the Funding pages. Students should also investigate funding opportunities available in their home countries for studying abroad.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Registrar’s Office provides detailed information on tuition and fees, including specific guidance for international students and visiting researchers. Incoming international PhD students, from September 2021 onward, pay the same tuition fees as domestic students.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Health insurance is mandatory for international students and their dependents through the University Health Insurance Plan (UHIP), which covers hospitals, doctors, and some diagnostic fees. Enrollment must occur within 30 days of arrival in Canada to avoid penalties. Additional supplementary health coverage may be available through the Society of Graduate & Professional Students (SGPS).

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The Registrar’s Office provides budgeting guidance, including estimated living and educational expenses for international master’s and doctoral students. The Grad Student Funding Guide also offers budgeting examples for students with or without dependents, which is particularly important for those bringing family members to Canada.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Proficiency in English is required for admission. Applicants whose native language is not English must achieve satisfactory results on an approved English Language Proficiency Test before acceptance. Detailed requirements are outlined in Step 4 of the How to Apply page.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: International students enrolled in programs longer than six months must obtain a Canadian study permit, and some may require a temporary resident visa. The Queen’s University International Centre (QUIC) provides guidance on immigration documentation, health insurance, housing, and related processes.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: BorderPass Immigration Support is available to graduate students at no cost as part of Queen’s commitment to international student success. This service connects students with trusted Canadian immigration lawyers through an AI-enabled platform, supporting study, work, and long-term immigration pathways throughout their time in Canada.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Queen’s University has launched the Queen’s Special U.S. Doctoral Recruitment Initiative in response to recent funding cuts in the United States. This initiative supports 20 doctoral students whose funded offers from top U.S. universities have been rescinded or who are reconsidering U.S. doctoral offers for the 2026/27 academic year. As a research-intensive institution, Queen’s provides a strong scholarly environment for PhD students to advance innovative research and global impact.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Selected candidates under the Special U.S. Doctoral Recruitment Initiative will receive a minimum of $40,000 CAD annually for four years of study, along with access to a fast-track admission process and extended admissions deadlines.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: The initiative is open to American, Canadian, and international students who have had a doctoral offer rescinded or are reconsidering a doctoral offer from a U.S. university ranked in the top 100 worldwide (QS or Times Higher Education) in an equivalent field, with a funding package. Applicants must demonstrate proof of admission and funding from the U.S. institution, secure supervision from a Queen’s faculty member, and meet all admission requirements of the intended Queen’s doctoral program.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Prospective applicants must first identify and obtain a commitment from a Queen’s faculty supervisor by providing a CV, transcript, statement of proposed research, and proof of eligibility. After securing supervision, applicants must complete the initiative intake form, specifying their intended program and submitting documentation of their prior U.S. doctoral offer and funding package. The School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs will review submissions and invite eligible candidates to complete a full application for adjudication and selection.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs

text: Only students who accept an offer from Queen’s University after April 27, 2025, will be considered for this Special Recruitment Initiative. Inquiries about the initiative should be directed to the School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs.

metadata.unit: School of Graduate Studies and Postdoctoral Affairs
